name: DPO
oneliner: Reparameterizes RLHF into a classification loss on preference pairs — no reward model, no PPO, 2 models in memory. The default open-source alignment method.
explanation: Optimal RLHF policy satisfies r(x,y) = $\beta$·log[$\pi_\theta$/$\pi_{\text{ref}}$] + Z(x). Substituting into Bradley-Terry cancels reward model — reduces to supervised loss. 2-5$\times$ cheaper than RLHF. Failure modes: distribution shift, overfitting to chosen, reference sensitivity. Default for open-source: Zephyr, Tulu 2, Llama 3.1, many community models.
fundamentals: Loss: $\mathcal{L} = -\mathbb{E}\left[\log \sigma\left(\beta \log \frac{\pi_\theta(y_w|x)}{\pi_{\text{ref}}(y_w|x)} - \beta \log \frac{\pi_\theta(y_l|x)}{\pi_{\text{ref}}(y_l|x)}\right)\right]$. $\beta$ typically 0.1-0.5. lr 1e-6 to 5e-7. Same preference data as RLHF RM.

foundational_papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
Zephyr: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment
A General Theoretical Paradigm to Understand Learning from Human Feedback (IPO)
